title: pentaschistis viscidula
intro: Pentaschistis viscidula, also known as sticky tussock grass, is a species of tussock grass native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pentaschistis viscidula include wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by making a tincture from the dried leaves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
